= Chapter 1: System Overview & Tech Stack

== 1.1 Product Description and Core Philosophy

Kosha is a mobile-first personal finance Progressive Web App that combines everyday money operations (transactions, bills, loans) with trust-oriented analytics and reconciliation. Architecturally, it is optimized for three outcomes:

. Fast perceived performance on consumer mobile devices.
. Strong data integrity with server-truth persistence.
. Operational resilience during auth refreshes, flaky networks, and runtime faults.

=== Core philosophy pillars

. Server truth, client acceleration
The source of truth is Supabase/Postgres; the UI accelerates perception through cache, prefetch, and staged invalidation rather than replacing canonical state.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/supabase.js#L13[src/lib/supabase.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/queryClient.js#L3[src/lib/queryClient.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L535[src/App.jsx]

. Latency hiding without correctness compromise
Loading skeletons, route-level suspense, and prefetch are used so transitions feel instant, but writes still await backend completion before stabilization.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L147[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L94[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L807[src/App.jsx]

. Auth-first stability
Auth bootstrap and token refresh race conditions are explicitly handled with a module-level auth store and guarded initialization paths.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L78[src/hooks/useAuth.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L101[src/hooks/useAuth.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/authStore.js#L1[src/lib/authStore.js]

. Fail-soft runtime posture
Global runtime monitoring and error boundaries capture crash context and keep bug-report flow actionable.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L9[src/main.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L1[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js#L52[src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js]

. Design-system consistency over ad hoc styling
Tailwind tokens, CSS variables, shared animation semantics, and standardized sheet/navigation primitives enforce visual coherence across pages.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L6[tailwind.config.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L60[src/index.css], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L486[src/index.css]

'''

== 1.2 Complete Tech Stack Breakdown

|===
| Layer | Technology | How Kosha Uses It | Architectural Implication
| UI runtime | React 18 | Root rendering under StrictMode with composable providers and lazy route boundaries. | Double-invocation safety and effect discipline are required; startup code is written to be idempotent.
| Routing | React Router 6 | BrowserRouter + route-level guards + auth callback + fallback routing. | Route security and onboarding gating are centralized and deterministic.
| Build system | Vite 5 + plugin-react | Fast dev server, ESM build, rollup manual chunking by dependency families. | Smaller initial payload via chunk segmentation; predictable vendor split for cache efficiency.
| PWA | vite-plugin-pwa (Workbox) | Auto-update service worker, manifest, runtime caching policies, Supabase REST cache bucket. | Offline friendliness and repeat-visit speed; requires careful cache invalidation strategy.
| Styling | Tailwind CSS 3 + CSS tokens | Semantic palette, radius/typography scales, component classes, safe-area variables. | Design consistency is encoded at config level; new UI should consume tokens, not ad hoc values.
| Animation | Framer Motion | Page transitions, bottom-nav interaction physics, modal/sheet motion choreography, presence transitions. | Motion is structural (state transition communication), not cosmetic only.
| Accessible overlays | Radix UI Dialog | Dialog primitives for destructive confirms and sheet-like interactions. | Accessibility semantics (focus trap, portal behavior) are standardized and reusable.
| Data client | Supabase JS v2 | Auth state machine, PostgREST CRUD, RPC invocations, Realtime channels. | Backend contracts are directly consumed in frontend hooks; schema changes must be coordinated.
| Query orchestration | TanStack React Query v5 | Global SWR policy, retry heuristics, invalidation families, prefetch pipelines. | Data freshness and performance are controlled centrally, reducing page-level drift.
| Charting | Recharts | Analytics and dashboard visualization components. | Data shaping at hook/lib layer is essential; chart layer assumes normalized structures.
|===

== 1.3 App Initialization Flow (main.jsx -> App.jsx -> AuthGuard)

This flow is the most important operational path for every developer to understand.

=== Phase A: Bootstrap and global safety net

. Runtime diagnostics listeners are registered before React mount.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L9[src/main.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js#L52[src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js]

. The React root renders inside StrictMode and wraps the full app in a global error boundary.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L11[src/main.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L13[src/main.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L1[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx]

Practical meaning:
- Unexpected render crashes are intercepted globally.
- Crash context can be forwarded into bug reporting.

=== Phase B: Core application composition in App

. Router is established first with future flags for transition compatibility.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L914[src/App.jsx]

. AuthProvider is mounted outside QueryClientProvider.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L916[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/context/AuthContext.jsx#L6[src/context/AuthContext.jsx]

. QueryClientProvider injects the singleton query client for all route trees.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L917[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/queryClient.js#L3[src/lib/queryClient.js]

. Global background services boot immediately after providers:
- Realtime sync daemon with fallback polling and reconnect backoff.
- Dashboard warm prefetch for first-view responsiveness.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L535[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L729[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L918[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L919[src/App.jsx]

. AppShell mounts structural UI:
- Runtime route tracker
- Custom category loader
- Desktop sidebar / mobile nav
- Animated route outlet
- Query error recovery bar
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L899[src/App.jsx]

=== Phase C: Auth bootstrap state machine

The auth state machine is implemented inside useAuthState and exposed through context.

. Initial local state starts as loading true and profileLoading true.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L12[src/hooks/useAuth.js]

. Supabase onAuthStateChange subscription handles lifecycle events:
- INITIAL_SESSION
- TOKEN_REFRESHED
- SIGNED_IN
- SIGNED_OUT
- USER_UPDATED
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L78[src/hooks/useAuth.js]

. On INITIAL_SESSION:
- authStore is updated first to eliminate cold-start mutation race.
- user state is set.
- loading is released.
- profile fetch begins if user exists.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L82[src/hooks/useAuth.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/authStore.js#L1[src/lib/authStore.js]

. Safety timer releases loading after 3 seconds if INITIAL_SESSION fails to fire.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L143[src/hooks/useAuth.js]

. Context value is memoized to prevent broad rerender cascades.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/context/AuthContext.jsx#L18[src/context/AuthContext.jsx]

=== Phase D: Route resolution and guard enforcement

. Routes are lazy-loaded and wrapped in suspense skeletons per route family.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L31[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L807[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L147[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx]

. Protected routes are wrapped with AuthGuard.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L815[src/App.jsx]

. AuthGuard logic:
- If auth/profile still loading: show route-matched skeleton.
- If no user: imperative navigate to login.
- If user without onboarded profile: navigate to onboarding.
- Else: render children.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L157[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L169[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L171[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx]

. Auth callback route resolves post-OAuth/email-link landing and redirects by auth/profile state.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L519[src/App.jsx]

'''

== 1.4 Initialization Sequence (Operational View)

. Browser loads bundle built by Vite and chunk-split by dependency families.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/vite.config.js#L5[vite.config.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/vite.config.js#L48[vite.config.js]

. main.jsx starts runtime monitor and mounts React root under global boundary.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L9[src/main.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L11[src/main.jsx]

. App mounts router and providers, starts realtime and prefetch workers.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L914[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L535[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L729[src/App.jsx]

. useAuthState receives INITIAL_SESSION, normalizes user/profile, hydrates context.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L82[src/hooks/useAuth.js]

. Route-level suspense displays skeleton while lazy chunks and guard decisions resolve.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L807[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L147[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx]

. AuthGuard either redirects or unlocks protected route subtree.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L157[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx]

. User reaches stable interactive state with nav, realtime freshness, and query cache active.

'''

== 1.5 Architectural Notes for New Engineers

. Auth race hardening is intentional and must not be removed casually.
The authStore bridge is a deliberate fix for cold-start token refresh windows.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/authStore.js#L1[src/lib/authStore.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L82[src/hooks/useAuth.js]

. Realtime is treated as freshness enhancer, not source of truth.
If websocket fails, the system degrades to periodic invalidation with reconnect backoff.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L532[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L535[src/App.jsx]

. Suspense + route skeletons are part of perceived-performance architecture.
Do not replace with blank loading states unless UX regression is acceptable.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L62[src/App.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L147[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx]

. Styling and motion are token-driven and componentized.
Prefer extending shared tokens/classes over inline one-off values.
Evidence: link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L6[tailwind.config.js], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L60[src/index.css]
